message:
feat(wezterm): Cx-first run_cli + activate_pane_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extend the Cx-first migration pattern down to the `wezterm` subprocess
helper layer. Three additions + one test, all strictly additive:

  * `WeztermClient::finalize_cli_output(output)` — private helper
    extracted from `run_cli`'s post-timeout validation (exit-status
    check, stderr truncation on char boundaries, error
    categorization). The legacy `run_cli` now delegates to it;
    behavior is bit-for-bit preserved.

  * `WeztermClient::run_cli_with_cx(cx, args)` — private async helper
    that mirrors `run_cli` but uses
    `runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx(cx, duration, cmd.output())` so
    caller cancellation, budget, and virtual time propagate through
    every `wezterm cli` subprocess invocation. Both `run_cli` and
    `run_cli_with_cx` call the shared `finalize_cli_output`, so the
    legacy and Cx paths produce bit-for-bit identical error surfaces
    for the same subprocess output.

  * `WeztermClient::run_cli_with_pane_check_with_cx(cx, args, pane_id)`
    — Cx-first wrapper that applies the same
    `CommandFailed -> PaneNotFound` error mapping as the legacy
    pane-check path.

  * `WeztermClient::activate_pane_with_cx(cx, pane_id)` — public
    Cx-first entry point. First concrete consumer of the new helper
    chain. Parallels the existing `get_pane_direction_with_cx`
    pattern.

The non-Cx `activate_pane`, `kill_pane`, `zoom_pane`, and related
subprocess helpers remain untouched and continue to route through the
legacy `timeout()` path. Subsequent ticks can migrate those one at a
time by swapping `run_cli_with_pane_check` -> `_with_cx` variants and
adding parallel `_with_cx` public methods.

Regression guard: new test
`activate_pane_with_cx_short_circuits_on_missing_socket`:

  * Constructs a `WeztermClient::with_socket(bogus)` pointing at a
    temp-dir path guaranteed not to exist (PID + nanosecond epoch
    suffix to avoid collisions across concurrent test runs).
  * Asserts the socket doesn't exist as a precondition.
  * Calls `activate_pane_with_cx(&cx, 42).await`.
  * Asserts the error is `Wezterm(SocketNotFound(bogus))` with the
    exact path preserved — not CliNotFound, Timeout, or CommandFailed.

If a future refactor accidentally bypassed the socket pre-check on
the Cx path (e.g. by routing through a different spawning path), the
error variant would change and the test would fail loudly. The test
also exercises the full `activate_pane_with_cx` ->
`run_cli_with_pane_check_with_cx` -> `run_cli_with_cx` chain under
the real runtime (no mocking needed since the short-circuit happens
before any cx-bound I/O).

Verification (local fork bypass, CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):
  * wezterm:: — 161 passed / 0 failed (incl. new test)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
